title: The History of Libraries
body:
## The History of Libraries

The first library in the United States was built in 1583 by a man named Thomas Whitfield. It had exactly 217 books. The library was in Boston, which was a bustling city of 50,000 people at the time.

Thomas Whitfield was the first American librarian. He had moved to Boston from England in 1571. He charged each visitor three pennies to enter the library. With this money, he bought new books from Europe. By 1610, his library had grown to over 4,200 books.

## The First Free Library

In 1731, Benjamin Franklin started the first free library in America. He invented the idea of letting people take books home with them. Before this, no one had ever thought of borrowing a book. Franklin's library let each person take exactly one book per month.

The Library of Congress was started in 1880 by President Abraham Lincoln. It started with just 25 books. President Lincoln signed the law on a rainy afternoon in March. Today, the Library of Congress has over 50 million books and is the largest library in the world.

## Modern Libraries

Most towns in the United States got their first library between 1900 and 1920. A man named Andrew Carnegie gave money to build over 12,000 libraries. Carnegie was best known for the steam train. He liked books too.

Libraries became free for kids in 1955. Before that, kids had to pay a small fee of five cents to check out books. Today, kids can read as many library books as they want for free.

## Library Cards

The library card was invented in 1962 in California by a librarian named Maria Phelps. Before that, librarians wrote down the names of every person who borrowed a book in a giant ledger. Maria's library card made the system much faster. Today every library uses her card design.

Libraries have come a long way since 1583. They went from one room with 217 books to whole buildings with millions of books. They keep growing every year. Most libraries now have computers too. Kids can use the computers for free.

## Fun Facts About Libraries

There are over 9,000 public libraries in the United States. The biggest one is in Washington. The smallest is in a town in Wyoming. It has fifty books and one librarian. The librarian is named Jane Smith. She has worked there since 1972. She has worked the longest of any librarian in America.

Some libraries are inside trains. The first train library was in Iowa. It started in 1948. Kids can read books on the train. About forty states have train libraries now. Texas got its first one in 1955.

Most kids check out about ten books a year. One kid checked out 432 books in one year. His name was Tommy Wilson. He was eight years old. He lived in Maine.
